title: Move get_entity tool tests from test_health_endpoint.py to dedicated test_tools.py
analysis: Task 129 appended two get_entity MCP tool tests to fused-memory/tests/test_health_endpoint.py (lines 644-687): test_get_entity_tool_passes_valid_only_true_to_service and test_get_entity_tool_valid_only_defaults_to_false. These test the get_entity tool handler's valid_only parameter forwarding, not any health endpoint. They are fully self-contained — each creates its own AsyncMock service and MCP server inline, with no dependency on the file's fixtures (mcp_server, mcp_server_with_service, etc.). The move is clean: create test_tools.py with the two tests and their imports, then remove the section (lines 644-687) from test_health_endpoint.py. No fixture sharing needed. The new file name test_tools.py parallels test_memory_service.py (service-layer tests vs tool-layer tests) and provides a natural home for future tool-level tests that are currently co-located in test_health_endpoint.py.

steps.description: Create fused-memory/tests/test_tools.py with the two get_entity tool tests (test_get_entity_tool_passes_valid_only_true_to_service, test_get_entity_tool_valid_only_defaults_to_false) copied from test_health_endpoint.py. Include the necessary imports (AsyncMock, pytest, create_mcp_server) and module docstring. Run pytest on the new file to confirm both tests pass.

Remove the get_entity section (section comment block at lines 644-646 and the two async test functions at lines 649-687) from test_health_endpoint.py. Run pytest on both test_health_endpoint.py and test_tools.py to confirm: (a) the old file's remaining tests still pass, (b) the new file's 2 tests still pass, and (c) no duplicate test names exist.

design_decisions.decision: Name the new file test_tools.py rather than test_get_entity_tool.py
design_decisions.rationale: test_tools.py parallels test_memory_service.py (service-layer vs tool-layer) and provides a natural landing zone for future tool-level tests that are currently misplaced in test_health_endpoint.py. Avoids file proliferation — one file per tool would create 15+ test files.

design_decisions.decision: Keep tests self-contained (no shared fixtures) in test_tools.py for now
design_decisions.rationale: The two get_entity tests already create their own mocks inline. Extracting fixtures is premature since the file starts with only 2 tests. Fixtures can be introduced when more tests are moved here in the future.

design_decisions.decision: Do not move other tool tests from test_health_endpoint.py in this task
design_decisions.rationale: The task scope is specifically the two get_entity tests. Moving others (update_task, set_task_status, delete_memory, add_memory, search, get_episodes, etc.) would be a scope violation — that's a separate cleanup task.

reuse.what: Existing test pattern from test_health_endpoint.py: AsyncMock service + create_mcp_server + _tool_manager.call_tool
reuse.how: Copy the exact test code verbatim — the tests are self-contained and use the same mock pattern already established in the file.
Import and use to create MCP server instances with mocked MemoryService for tool-level testing.
